description: Create a real enterprise calendar workspace package under `ee/packages/calendar`.
Create a real enterprise Microsoft Teams workspace package under `ee/packages/microsoft-teams`.
Choose non-colliding workspace and package names for the new EE calendar and Teams packages.
Add root index exports for the enterprise calendar package.
Add root index exports for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Add actions entrypoints for the enterprise calendar package.
Add actions entrypoints for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Add component entrypoints for the enterprise calendar package.
Add component entrypoints for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Add lib or service entrypoints for the enterprise calendar package.
Add lib or service entrypoints for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Register the enterprise calendar package in workspace metadata and project tooling.
Register the enterprise Microsoft Teams package in workspace metadata and project tooling.
Add package-local TypeScript config for the enterprise calendar package.
Add package-local TypeScript config for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Add package-local build scripts for the enterprise calendar package.
Add package-local build scripts for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Declare explicit runtime dependencies for the enterprise calendar package.
Declare explicit runtime dependencies for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Ensure React or UI peer dependency handling is valid for both new enterprise packages.
Move enterprise calendar actions out of `packages/ee` and into the enterprise calendar package.
Move the enterprise calendar integrations settings component into the enterprise calendar package.
Move the enterprise calendar profile settings component into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar provider services into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar sync service logic into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar webhook processor logic into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar webhook maintenance logic into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar provider adapter implementations into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar provider base abstractions into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar event bus subscriber logic into the enterprise calendar package.
Move enterprise calendar job-handler logic into the enterprise calendar package.
Expose Google calendar OAuth callback handler logic from the enterprise calendar package.
Expose Microsoft calendar OAuth callback handler logic from the enterprise calendar package.
Expose Google calendar webhook handler logic from the enterprise calendar package.
Expose Microsoft calendar webhook handler logic from the enterprise calendar package.
Expose thin server-consumable calendar route handler entrypoints from the enterprise calendar package.
Expose thin UI-consumable calendar component entrypoints from the enterprise calendar package.
Remove duplicated live calendar logic split between `packages/ee` and `ee/server`.
Remove deep filesystem imports from enterprise calendar code to `ee/server` implementation files.
Make the enterprise calendar package the single source of truth for live enterprise calendar behavior.
Move enterprise Teams actions out of `packages/ee` and into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams package-management actions into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move the enterprise Teams integrations settings component into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams notification delivery logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams auth and provider-resolution helpers into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams bot handler logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams message-extension handler logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams quick-action handler logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams tab access and auth state logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams tenant and linked-user resolution logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams deep-link helpers into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Move enterprise Teams action registry logic into the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams bot route handler entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams message-extension route handler entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams auth-callback route handler entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams package-management route handler entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams quick-action route handler entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Expose Teams tab page and server helper entrypoints from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Remove duplicated live Teams logic split between `packages/ee` and `ee/server`.
Make the enterprise Microsoft Teams package the single source of truth for live enterprise Teams behavior.
Rewire EE Google calendar OAuth route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE Microsoft calendar OAuth route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE Google calendar webhook route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE Microsoft calendar webhook route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE calendar settings entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE calendar profile entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire EE Teams bot route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.

Rewire EE Teams auth callback entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Rewire EE Teams package-management route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Rewire EE Teams quick-action route entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Rewire EE Teams tab page entrypoints in `ee/server` to delegate to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Rewire any remaining EE server helper ownership for calendar to the enterprise calendar package.
Rewire any remaining EE server helper ownership for Teams to the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Keep `ee/server` as a thin framework entrypoint layer rather than a second live implementation tree for calendar.
Keep `ee/server` as a thin framework entrypoint layer rather than a second live implementation tree for Teams.
Rewire shared calendar actions to the new enterprise calendar package entrypoints.
Rewire shared calendar settings wrappers to the new enterprise calendar package entrypoints.
Rewire shared calendar profile wrappers to the new enterprise calendar package entrypoints.
Rewire shared Teams actions to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package entrypoints.
Rewire shared Teams package actions to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package entrypoints.
Rewire shared Teams settings wrappers to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package entrypoints.
Rewire shared Teams notification wrappers to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package entrypoints.
Rewire shared Teams auth and provider-resolution wrappers to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package entrypoints.

Reduce `packages/ee` Teams route files to CE stubs or temporary forwarders only.
Reduce `packages/ee` calendar UI files to CE stubs or temporary forwarders only.
Reduce `packages/ee` Teams UI files to CE stubs or temporary forwarders only.
Reduce `packages/ee` calendar lib and action files to CE stubs or temporary forwarders only.
Reduce `packages/ee` Teams lib and action files to CE stubs or temporary forwarders only.
Remove any remaining shared deep imports into `ee/server` implementation files for calendar.
Remove any remaining shared deep imports into `ee/server` implementation files for Teams.
Update base TypeScript pathing so `packages/ee` is no longer the live source of truth for calendar and Teams enterprise code.
Update server TypeScript pathing for the new enterprise calendar package.
Update server TypeScript pathing for the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Update EE Next.js aliasing so calendar resolves to the new enterprise calendar package.
Update EE Next.js aliasing so Teams resolves to the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Keep CE Next.js aliasing fail-closed for calendar after the package move.
Keep CE Next.js aliasing fail-closed for Teams after the package move.
Update Vitest aliasing so calendar tests resolve consistently to the intended live or stub sources.
Update Vitest aliasing so Teams tests resolve consistently to the intended live or stub sources.
Include the enterprise calendar package in workspace build, lint, and typecheck targets.
Include the enterprise Microsoft Teams package in workspace build, lint, and typecheck targets.
Ensure CI dependency graph and task execution recognize the new enterprise calendar package.
Ensure CI dependency graph and task execution recognize the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Prevent new circular dependencies between shared packages, `ee/server`, and the new enterprise packages.
Remove or collapse obsolete duplicated calendar files after the package move.
Remove or collapse obsolete duplicated Teams files after the package move.
Update source comments and ownership notes that still describe `packages/ee` as live calendar implementation.
Update source comments and ownership notes that still describe `packages/ee` as live Teams implementation.
Document the new enterprise calendar package location in engineer-facing runbooks or scratchpad notes.
Document the new enterprise Microsoft Teams package location in engineer-facing runbooks or scratchpad notes.
Add a passing package-level typecheck target for the enterprise calendar package.
Add a passing package-level typecheck target for the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Keep server typecheck passing after the new enterprise package graph is introduced.
Keep targeted calendar route and component tests passing against the new package layout.
Keep targeted Teams route, action, and notification tests passing against the new package layout.
Keep CE stub contract tests passing for calendar after the relocation.
Keep CE stub contract tests passing for Teams after the relocation.
Eliminate remaining live calendar import paths that depend on `packages/ee` as the primary implementation root.
Eliminate remaining live Teams import paths that depend on `packages/ee` as the primary implementation root.
Leave unrelated enterprise domains in `packages/ee` untouched unless required by calendar or Teams dependencies.
